200.description: Login successful - returns user object with tokens
Tokens refreshed successfully - returns user object with new tokens

post.summary: Refresh access token using refresh token

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query - searches title, client name, property address, and job ref

parameters.name: createdFrom
parameters.description: Filter surveys created on or after this date (ISO 8601)

parameters.name: createdTo
parameters.description: Filter surveys created on or before this date (ISO 8601)

200.description: Paginated list of surveys matching search criteria

get.summary: Search and list surveys with pagination and filtering

post.summary: Create a new survey with sections and answers

get.summary: Get a survey by ID with all sections and answers

put.summary: Update a survey with sections and answers

delete.summary: Soft delete a survey (marks as deleted)

post.summary: Create a new section in a survey
Create a new answer in a section

post.description: Upload a photo, audio recording, or signature to a survey. File is stored locally and metadata is saved to database.

surveyId.description: Survey UUID to attach media to

get.description: Retrieve metadata for a media file. Does not return the file itself.

delete.description: Hard delete a media file. Removes both the file from storage and the database record.

get.description: Stream the actual media file. Sets appropriate Content-Type header.

post.description: Accepts a batch of operations from the client offline queue. Processes operations transactionally with idempotency support. Duplicate batches (same idempotencyKey) return cached response.

get.description: Returns entities that have changed since the provided timestamp. Use the returned serverTimestamp as the since parameter for subsequent pulls. Includes surveys, sections, answers, and media metadata owned by the user.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp to fetch changes after (exclusive)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of changes to return (1-500, default 100)

info.title: SurveyScriber API
info.description:
SurveyScriber Backend API - Professional Field Survey & Inspection Platform

## Authentication
This API uses JWT Bearer tokens. Obtain tokens via `/auth/login` and include in the Authorization header.

## Offline-First Architecture
The API supports granular CRUD operations for surveys, sections, and answers to enable offline-first Flutter clients to sync individual changes without full survey uploads.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: User authentication: login, register, refresh tokens

tags.name: Sections
tags.description: Section CRUD - granular section management for sync

tags.name: Answers
tags.description: Answer CRUD - granular answer management for sync

password.description: Password (min 8 chars, must include uppercase, lowercase, number)

expiresIn.description: Access token expiration time in seconds

refreshToken.description: Refresh token from login or previous refresh

hasNext.description: Whether there is a next page

hasPrev.description: Whether there is a previous page

order.description: Order of the section within the survey

type.description: Survey type (LEVEL_2, LEVEL_3, VALUATION, SNAGGING, REINSPECTION, OTHER)

value.example: Updated: Good condition with minor wear

order.description: Order of the section within the survey

sections.description: Sections within the survey (replaces existing sections)

operationId.description: Client-generated unique ID for this operation (for idempotency)

clientTimestamp.description: ISO timestamp when operation was created on client

idempotencyKey.description: Client-generated idempotency key for the entire batch

operations.description: Array of sync operations to apply

entityId.description: Server-side entity ID (may differ from client for new entities)

duplicate.description: Whether this operation was a duplicate (idempotent replay)

serverTimestamp.description: Server timestamp when sync was processed

batchDuplicate.description: Whether entire batch was replayed (duplicate idempotency key)

updatedAt.description: When entity was last updated on server

serverTimestamp.description: Server timestamp for this response (use as since for next pull)

hasMore.description: Whether there are more changes to fetch

totalCount.description: Total count of changes matching query
